channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: IRS Commissioner Daniel Werfel says he'll step down on Trump's Inauguration Day
item.description: IRS Commissioner Daniel Werfel says he intends to step down from his position on President-elect Donald Trump’s Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump swearing-in to move indoors due to cold weather, source tells Fox News
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance will be sworn into office indoors because of wintery conditions expected in Washington, D.C., on Monday, a source tells Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury levels sanctions tied to a massive hack of telecom companies and breach of its own network
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department on Friday announced sanctions in connection with a massive Chinese hack of American telecommunications companies and a breach of its own computer network.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Senate advances a migrant detention bill that could be Trump's first law to sign
item.description: The Senate is heading toward a final vote on legislation that would require federal authorities to detain migrants accused of theft and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Walz offers up tighter budget blueprint amid abnormal start to session
item.description: The 2025 Minnesota Legislature got a dose of normalcy when Gov. Tim Walz unveiled his blueprint for the next two-year budget. The governor also weighed in for the first time publicly on some of the chaos in the Minnesota House.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Austin, the first Black defense secretary, ends his term marred by Afghanistan but buoyed by Ukraine
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin is bidding farewell to the forces and personnel he's led through a tumultuous term.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Army expects to meet recruiting goals, in dramatic turnaround, and denies 'wokeness' is a factor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army expects to meet its enlistment goals for 2025, marking a dramatic turnaround for a service that has struggled for several years to bring in enough young people and has undergone a major overhaul of its recruiting programs.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump DHS pick Noem likely to face scrutiny over deportation, border plans at confirmation hearing
item.description: President-elect Trump's pick to lead the Department of Homeland Security will face her confirmation hearing on Wednesday, where illegal immigration is likely to be a top topic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kamala Harris made history as vice president. The rest didn't go as planned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With Donald Trump’s return to the White House only days away, Kamala Harris ' staff packed into her ceremonial office to watch her sign the desk, a tradition performed by her predecessors for decades.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Key moments from Kristi Noem's hearing for Homeland Security chief
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem is promising to be a tough hand in carrying out President-elect Donald Trump's vision for the sprawling Department of Homeland Security including his plans to crack down on border security.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana And Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No taxes on tips comes closer to reality with Cortez Masto, Rosen push
item.description: No taxes on tips is becoming a closer reality for millions of workers after Democratic Nevada Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen and other senators reintroduced the proposal Thursday, her office said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis picks Florida AG Ashley Moody to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: Her support for President-elect Donald Trump and legal background is poised to make her a strong ally in the upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Senate adds amendment to immigration bill named after Laken Riley
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday adopted the first amendment to the Laken Riley Act, as Republicans push for a legislative win to open the new Congress. The legislation in its current form would mandate federal detention of immigrants without legal status accused of theft, burglary and other related crimes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Live Updates: Trump nominees grilled by senators; Turner ouster draws bipartisan ire
item.description: Thursday marked another busy day of hearings for President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees as his inauguration approaches.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: A need for speed: Several states are looking for ways to count votes faster
item.description: Lawmakers in California and other states want to change vote-counting rules to speed up the process. One key question is whether counting can be sped up without sacrificing access to the ballot.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez, MPR News

item.title: Walz unveils slimmer Minnesota budget, pitches sales tax cut
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz on Thursday pitched a tighter two-year budget, complete with spending pullbacks and other measures to keep a potential deficit at bay. He also proposed a lower state sales tax rate but called for extending the tax’s reach to financial services.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok ban to impact local businesses, content creators
item.description: San Diego is home to talented and creative content creators who make a living on TikTok. While many are able to make a full-time income on the app, some are also helping small businesses in return, who say the app has inadvertently helped them tremendously.
item.dc:creator: Zara Barker, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: “Kids can get online porn through gaming systems, tablets, phones, computers. Let me just say that content filtering for all those different devices, I can say from personal experience, is difficult to keep up with,” said Justice Amy Coney Barrett, who has seven children.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: NYC mayor: More beds for mentally ill people, more cops on subways
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- With more NYPD officers heading into the subway system during the overnight hours, New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he is also looking to get mentally ill homeless people off the trains with a new option.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Flags ordered to fly at full-staff in multiple states on Inauguration Day
item.description: The 30-day mourning period, reserved for the death of a sitting or former president, would have overlapped with the inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip the inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Live coverage: Biden's farewell speech comes on heels of raucous confirmation hearings
item.description: President Biden delivered a somewhat grim primetime farewell speech on Wednesday from the Oval Office to mark the final days of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DFLer Doron Clark, Republican Abigail Wolters win primary in Senate District 60 special election
item.description: A special primary election Tuesday narrowed the field of candidates running for a Minnesota Senate seat from nine to two.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Few US adults confident Justice Department and FBI will act fairly under Trump, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, prepares for questioning from senators on Wednesday, only about 2 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Departing DHS Secretary Mayorkas contends he delivered border security in the end
item.description: As DHS Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas prepares to leave office, NPR sits down for an exit interview. He tells us the border is more secure now than before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Milton Guevara and Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota legislative standoff spills out of the Statehouse into the courthouse
item.description: With accusations flying over which party is ignoring state law, the fight to control the Minnesota House is shifting from lawmakers to lawyers. DFL Secretary of State Steve Simon is calling on the Minnesota Supreme Court to weigh in on who has what power.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Hochul's tax cut plan: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to cut taxes for more than 8.3 million taxpayers in New York. The proposal was announced as part of her 2025 State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: ACLU sues Gov. DeSantis on behalf of Florida voters over special elections
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union is suing Gov. Ron DeSantis for not calling special elections for state House and Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
